architecture.summary: ExMRD is an explainable micro-video rumor detection framework that uses a Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) guided by a novel R3CoT (Refining, Retrieving, Reasoning on MVRD) inference mechanism to reorganize video content, retrieve domain knowledge about rumors, and generate explanations. A smaller trained language model (SLReviewer) distills the MLLM outputs to produce efficient and reliable predictions with rationales, avoiding direct fine-tuning of the expensive MLLM.

biblio.title: Following Clues, Approaching the Truth: Explainable Micro-Video Rumor Detection via Chain-of-Thought Reasoning

claims.statement: ExMRD significantly outperforms competitive baselines while providing high-quality rationales

R3CoT mechanism enables Multimodal Large Language Models to reorganize the original video content, retrieve domain knowledge related to rumors, and generate explainable conclusions regarding whether the micro-video contains rumor information
SLReviewer distills the outputs of R3CoT guided MLLMs to ensure efficient and reliable predictions
Existing Micro-Video Rumor Detection methods are generally black-box, which lacks transparency and makes it difficult to understand the reasoning behind classification decisions
